spec.description: Organization-level health, API usage, licensing, and assurance alerts.

Rolling-window request count gauge from the API — NOT a counter, rendered as-is (same F-152 windowed-gauge principle). Legend uses org_id (see panel 9 for why the org_info join is avoided on range queries).
spec.title: API Request Count

spec.description: Status label values discovered live (fixes F-155: co-term shows OK/expired-style; per-device shows active/expired/expiring/recentlyQueued/unused/unusedActive — no grace_period value exists)
spec.title: License Status Distribution

spec.expr: sum by (org_name, license_type, status) (meraki_org_licenses{job="meraki_dashboard", org_id=~"$organization"} * on(org_id) group_left(org_name) meraki_org_info{job="meraki_dashboard"})

spec.title: License Summary by Organization and Type

spec.expr: topk(10, sum by (network_id) (meraki_alerts_by_network{job="meraki_dashboard", org_id=~"$organization"}) * on(network_id) group_left(network_name) meraki_network_info{job="meraki_dashboard"})

spec.description: bargauge rendered a degenerate single-item bar with a single network in scope; table is more reliable at any cardinality
spec.title: Top 10 Networks by Alert Count

spec.description: Filters org_id with =~ regex, never exact = (fixes F-154: exact match breaks multi-select/All)
spec.title: Active Alert Details

spec.description: org_id -> org_name join source (fixes F-150: real series is meraki_org_info, not meraki_org)
spec.title: Organizations

spec.description: 1-hour-window gauge from the Meraki API (fixes F-152: rendered as-is, NOT wrapped in rate()/increase()). Legend uses org_id, not a meraki_org_info join — the join is stable for instant queries (see the Organizations table) but returns empty results for range/timeseries queries wider than ~2h, likely because meraki_org_info is emitted on a slower collector cadence than the range query's evaluation step and a same-step join miss on one org drops the whole vector.
spec.title: Organization Bandwidth Usage
Meraki – Organization
